title: artemisia alba subsp. kabylica
intro: Artemisia alba subsp. kabylica, also known as White Mugwort, is a subspecies of the Mugwort plant, belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Artemisia alba subsp. kabylica are used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, White Mugwort is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
